basics.name: Moris David Allan Arias
basics.title: Software Developer
basics.summary: Software Developer focused on backend engineering and enterprise process optimization. Specialized in implementing ERP systems (Openbravo, Odoo) and automation using N8N. Experienced in the full development lifecycle, PostgreSQL databases, and Docker container deployment, dedicated to designing robust and scalable solutions.

description: Developed and implemented backend customizations for ERP ecosystems (Openbravo and Odoo) using Java and Python.
Designed automated workflows with N8N for API integration and operational load reduction.
Optimized SQL queries in PostgreSQL, improving the performance of enterprise modules.
Developed web applications under the Java EE standard for internal affairs management.
Refactored Java code and migrated visual components to improve system stability and scalability.
Designed relational database schemas, ensuring the integrity of critical information.
Led a 2-person team in developing desktop systems using Java and PostgreSQL, overseeing all project phases from planning to deployment.
Developed technical data sheet and inventory management systems for an optician, including billing, stock control, lens specifications, and features for creation, update, and printing.
Coordinated with the client to define software requirements and trained the team in technologies like Java and PostgreSQL to enhance their technical skills.

projects.name: Pet Store e-commerce
projects.notes: Local deployment configured with SQLite for testing purposes. Developed using Django and Python.
overview: The Pet Store e-commerce project is an online platform designed to provide a seamless shopping experience for pet owners. It features an extensive catalog of pet products, user authentication, and shopping cart functionality.

The platform includes administrative functions for managing the inventory, updating product details, and tracking customer interactions. Built with Django and Python, it ensures a reliable and scalable backend architecture tailored for retail operations.

projects.name: Lens technical data sheet
projects.notes: Desktop inventory management and technical data sheet system developed for an optician.
overview: This specialized software is engineered to generate comprehensive technical data sheets for optical lenses, capturing every critical specification. These sheets are vital for the primary optical laboratory, allowing them to streamline the distribution of lenses to associated retail stores. The application incorporates a full CRUD system to manage these technical documents, organizing them in accessible tables where they can be easily printed for customer deliveries or exported to Excel for flexible data sharing.

Beyond product management, the system features a dedicated module for handling customer profiles with its own independent CRUD capabilities, ensuring the optical business can keep client information perfectly organized and up-to-date. Powerful search and filtering tools are built-in, enabling users to quickly locate specific technical sheets or customer records. By combining precise documentation, efficient data handling, and an intuitive interface, this application significantly improves sales operations and client management.

projects.name: Lens glass inventory
projects.notes: Inventory management system for lens glasses with detailed tracking and Excel export.
overview: Developed in Java, this inventory system is designed specifically to manage and track optical lenses. It enables users to register, edit, and monitor inventory items with comprehensive details like manufacturer, base curve, index, material, and quantity. Robust data validation is integrated to maintain database reliability, while the user interface instantly updates to show the latest stock status.

The application includes intuitive data tables that provide a complete snapshot of the available inventory and product specifications. This makes it straightforward for staff to keep records current. The software accommodates both the creation of new entries and the modification of existing ones, featuring safety prompts to avoid unintended edits.

Furthermore, the software automatically logs all inventory movements, capturing exact timestamps and transaction details. This functionality facilitates long-term stock tracking and simplifies inventory planning. It also provides a practical feature to export all data directly into Excel spreadsheets.
